subject: Public Health
title: Harm Reduction: Saving Lives Without Judgment
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Harm Reduction: Saving Lives Without Judgment.

hook.text: Harm reduction says: if you can't stop using, let's keep you alive until you can. It's pragmatic, not permissive.

buildup.text: Needle exchange programs, naloxone distribution, and supervised consumption sites all reduce death without requiring.

discovery.text: Needle exchanges reduce HIV among people who inject drugs by up to 50%—and increase engagement with treatment services.

twist.text: Critics call harm reduction 'enabling'—but evidence shows it saves lives and is a gateway to treatment, not to more.

climax.text: Harm reduction is endorsed by the WHO, UNAIDS, and the UN—based on decades of evidence from dozens of countries.

punchline.text: Dead people can't recover. Keeping them alive is always the first step.

quiz.question: By how much do needle exchange programs reduce HIV among injecting drug users?
